title: Laminated Vehicle Glazing with Zoned Conductive Heating Layer

sections.abstract: A laminated vehicle glazing uses a transparent conductive heating layer with local sensor feedback to de-ice priority visibility zones.
sections.claims: 1. A laminated vehicle windshield comprising an outer glass ply, an inner glass ply, a polymer interlayer between the glass plies, a transparent conductive heating layer embedded adjacent to the polymer interlayer, edge busbars electrically coupled to the conductive heating layer, and a controller configured to energize only a predefined wiper-rest heating zone. 2. The windshield of claim 1, wherein temperature sensors positioned near the heating zone provide feedback for closed-loop de-icing control.
sections.description: The glazing assembly is intended for winter operation. A conductive mesh or transparent conductive layer is positioned between the plies. Edge busbars deliver power while local thermal sensors allow the controller to limit energy to the lower wipe area. The arrangement reduces ice accumulation without fully heating the windshield.

title: Sensor Mounting Assembly for Vehicle Windshield

sections.abstract: A windshield-mounted sensor assembly improves alignment and moisture protection for rain and light sensors.
sections.claims: 1. A windshield sensor mounting assembly comprising a sensor mounting pad fixed to an inner glass surface, an adhesive layer positioned between the pad and the glass surface, alignment ribs extending from the mounting pad, and a moisture barrier surrounding the adhesive layer. 2. The assembly of claim 1, wherein the pad supports a detachable rain and light sensor bracket.
sections.description: The mounting pad forms a repeatable reference for optical sensors. The adhesive window is bordered by a barrier ring to limit moisture intrusion. Alignment ribs reduce placement variation during windshield assembly and simplify replacement of the sensor bracket.

sections.abstract: An acoustic windshield combines optical correction for a head-up display with cabin noise reduction.
sections.claims: 1. A laminated windshield comprising a wedge interlayer arranged in a driver viewing region, an acoustic damping layer laminated with the wedge interlayer, and an infrared reflective coating on one glass-facing surface. 2. The windshield of claim 1, wherein the wedge interlayer defines an optical correction zone configured for a projected head-up display image.
sections.description: The windshield balances display clarity and acoustic performance. The tapered interlayer corrects double imaging in the display zone while the acoustic damping layer attenuates broadband road noise. An infrared reflective coating lowers solar heat load without materially altering the optical correction zone.
